name: webhooks
context: GitHub webhooks — event-driven HTTP POST notifications for repository and organization events
info: GitHub webhooks deliver real-time HTTP POST notifications to external servers when events occur in a repository or organization. When a push, pull request, issue, release, or other event happens, GitHub sends a JSON payload to your configured URL. Webhooks complement [[github/actions]] by enabling external systems to respond to GitHub events without polling. Payload signatures (HMAC-SHA256) ensure authenticity. This reference covers webhook setup, event types, payload format, signature verification, and management. See [[github/auth]] for API authentication and [[github/repos/settings]] for webhook configuration on repositories.

sections.title: Webhook setup
sections.body: Create webhooks with `octokit.repos.createWebhook()` specifying `url`, `content_type` (json), `secret`, and `events`. Set `active: true` to enable delivery. The webhook URL must be publicly accessible over HTTPS. GitHub sends a `ping` event on creation to verify connectivity. See [[github/auth]] for token setup and [[github/repos/settings]] for repo-level configuration.

sections.title: Webhook events
sections.body: Subscribe to specific events: `push`, `pull_request` (integrates with [[github/pulls]]), `issues` (integrates with [[github/issues]]), `create`, `delete`, `release`, `workflow_run` (from [[github/actions]]). Use `*` to receive all events. Choose only the events you need to minimize traffic and processing overhead.

sections.title: Payload format
sections.body: Payloads are JSON POST requests with headers: `X-GitHub-Event` (event type), `X-GitHub-Delivery` (unique delivery ID), `X-Hub-Signature-256` (HMAC signature). The body includes `action`, `repository`, `sender`, and event-specific data. Parse the `X-GitHub-Event` header to route to the correct handler.

sections.title: Security and signatures
sections.body: Always verify the `X-Hub-Signature-256` header using HMAC-SHA256 with your webhook secret. Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual()` to prevent timing attacks. Reject requests with missing or invalid signatures. Rotate secrets periodically. Never log or expose the webhook secret. Use `@octokit/webhooks` for built-in verification and typed event handling.
